_comment_top: R6 Pillar 7 / D-C-23 / task 069 — seed row for the ManagePinnedContextHandler chat tool. Surfaces a single LLM-visible 'manage_pinned_context(action, pinType, title, content?)' function that creates or deletes user-curated pinned-context items per FR-47. Voice command surface: 'remember X' → create user-preference; 'always X' → create system-rule; 'forget X' → delete by title match.
_comment_routing: Runtime invocation routes to the C# class via sprk_handlerclass = 'ManagePinnedContextHandler' (= nameof handler class). Auto-discovered via ToolFrameworkExtensions.AddToolHandlersFromAssembly (ADR-010) — ZERO new top-level DI line.
_comment_capability_router_pre_pass: Layer 0 voice command pre-pass in CapabilityRouter (task 069 addition) recognises the 'remember X' / 'forget X' / 'always X' patterns and selects the synthetic 'manage_pinned_context' capability so the LLM is biased toward invoking this tool. The router pre-pass + this seed row + the handler form the complete recognition pipeline.
_comment_placement_boundary: ADR-013 binding: the handler injects IPinnedContextRepository directly (BFF-internal memory plumbing) — NOT through a PublicContracts facade. The pinned-context repository is AI-internal memory plumbing per the 2026-05-20 refined ADR-013 boundary rule (no PublicContracts facade for AI-internal collaborators). Mirrors the direct-injection rationale that landed in tasks 067 (hierarchical composition) and 068 (matter-memory activation).
_comment_security: sprk_requiredcapability = null (intentional). The 'remember / forget / always' affordance is a default user voice command available in every chat session. The handler's own gates (TenantId + UserId required; closed action+pinType enums; pin content length caps via PinnedContextRepository) are the authorization surface.
_comment_tenant_isolation: ADR-014 binding: the chat session's TenantId is forwarded into every IPinnedContextRepository call (CreateAsync requires PinnedContextItem.TenantId; GetByUserAsync + DeleteAsync take tenantId as the partition key). Cross-tenant reads/writes are structurally impossible.
_comment_user_scope: Pinned context is USER-scoped — the chat session's UserId (sourced from the principal's 'oid' claim at SprkChatAgentFactory contextFactory build time per task 069 wiring) must be non-empty. Standalone chat without an authenticated user produces a UserId=null context, and the handler refuses with ValidationFailed + a clear diagnostic ('Authenticate the session before invoking this tool'). The repository contract requires non-empty UserId on CreateAsync.
_comment_adr015: ADR-015 (BINDING) data governance: telemetry logs handler name + decision (created | deleted | refused_not_found | refused_*) + action + pinType + title LENGTH + content PRESENCE + deterministic IDs + duration ONLY. NEVER the user-authored title body. NEVER the user-authored content body. NEVER user message text. The two counters (memory.pin_created, memory.pin_deleted) tag with tenantId + userId + sessionId + pinType + decision — all deterministic identifiers / enum-like discriminators per Tier-1 safe.
_comment_pin_type_choice: The 'matter-fact' pinType is exposed in the JSON Schema enum for completeness with PinType (model contract), but voice commands map only to 'user-preference' (remember) and 'system-rule' (always). 'matter-fact' pins are normally written through the Pinned Memory UI (task 070) which carries the explicit matter binding. The handler accepts 'matter-fact' on create but does NOT set MatterId (it's set to null) — the UI task 070 will own the matter-bound write surface.
_comment_delete_semantics: Delete is by (pinType, title) case-insensitive match against the user's existing pins (GetByUserAsync returns all pins for the (tenant, user) tuple; the handler filters by pinType + title). Match miss returns a structured 'refused_not_found' response (success=true; data.status='refused_not_found') — NOT an error — so the LLM can pattern-match in its next turn and ask the user to confirm the exact label. PinnedContextRepository.DeleteAsync is itself idempotent (no-op on missing), but the handler short-circuits BEFORE calling delete so the LLM gets actionable feedback.
_comment_description_loadbearing: sprk_description is LOAD-BEARING per NFR-12: the LLM uses the description text to decide WHEN to invoke this tool AND how to translate the user's natural-language voice command into structured arguments. The phrasing makes the three voice-command mappings explicit (remember → user-preference; always → system-rule; forget → delete-by-title) so the LLM authors the correct (action, pinType, title) tuple without further prompting.
_comment_consumption: Read by scripts/Seed-TypedHandlers.ps1. Idempotent — script queries by sprk_handlerclass + sprk_toolcode with sprk_name 'SYS-%' filter; UPSERTs.
sprk_description: Manage the user's persistent 'pinned memory' — the small set of always-in-context facts and preferences the assistant keeps across sessions. Use this when the user says 'remember X' (action=create, pinType=user-preference), 'always X' (action=create, pinType=system-rule), or 'forget X' (action=delete, pinType matches the existing pin; matched case-insensitively against the user's pins by title). Pinned items are user-curated memory; titles and content are user-authored and persisted verbatim. The 'matter-fact' pinType exists for completeness but is normally written through the Pinned Memory UI rather than via voice. Title is a short identifying label (≤200 chars); content carries the body (≤1000 chars; defaults to the title when omitted on create). On delete, only pinType + title are needed; content is ignored.
_comment_availableincontexts: 100000001 = Chat only. Pinned-context management is a chat-surface voice affordance — playbook nodes write to sprk_analysisoutput + sprk_workingdocument, not the user-scoped pinned-context tier. The pinned-context UI (task 070) will provide an alternate write surface but still through the chat-side BFF API endpoints.

sprk_jsonschema.title: ManagePinnedContextHandler Request
sprk_jsonschema.description: Argument shape for chat-driven invocation of the manage_pinned_context tool. Creates or deletes a PinnedContextItem via IPinnedContextRepository scoped to the chat session's (tenantId, userId).

action.description: Closed enum. 'create' adds a new pin; 'delete' removes an existing pin matched by (pinType, title) against the user's pins (case-insensitive title match).

pinType.description: Closed enum. Voice mapping: 'remember' → user-preference; 'always' → system-rule. 'matter-fact' is supported for completeness but is normally set via the Pinned Memory UI rather than voice. Required on both create and delete (delete uses pinType + title to identify the target).

title.description: Short user-facing label for the pin. On create, the LLM authors a concise label summarizing the voice command (e.g., 'Terse responses', 'Never reveal cost data'). On delete, the title is matched case-insensitively against existing pins of the same pinType.

content.description: Optional pin body. Only meaningful on create. When omitted on create, the handler defaults the content to the title text. Persisted verbatim — the LLM should author this carefully as the literal text that will be re-injected into future system prompts.

sprk_configuration._comment: No method-discriminator dispatch — this row exposes a single LLM-facing function. Empty object reserved for future per-row defaults (e.g., per-tenant 'auto-prune-after-N-days' policy for user-preference pins).
